advice:
Take Amon-Ra St. Brown (WR, DET) right now.

He's the highest-VORP non-RB on the board at 89.84, projecting 247.54 points, and he's your most urgent need — you have zero WRs filled with 2 WR slots and a FLEX still open. He's the WR4 by positional rank but arguably the safest floor in this class, with five straight seasons of 182+ points and no injury reported.

The survivability math is the real pressure. You have 5 picks until your next turn, and three of the four gap teams have already taken a WR. Justin Jefferson (VORP 61.64, ADP 15.80) is the next WR of consequence and is very likely gone before you pick again. After Jefferson, the board drops sharply to Nico Collins (44.52 VORP) and A.J. Brown (43.35 VORP) — that's a ~46-point VORP cliff from St. Brown. Waiting one round almost certainly costs you that gap.

On the TE question: McBride (VORP 70.85, ADP 20.70) and Bowers (VORP 67.25, ADP 20.60) are both elite and still available, but their ADPs suggest they're more likely to survive into your next pick than St. Brown is. Lock in the WR1 now and target whichever TE remains next round.

One flag: both St. Brown and Jefferson share a bye week 6, so if Jefferson somehow survives and you grab him next pick, you'd have a stacked bye. Not a dealbreaker, but worth knowing.
